name: Adoption dynamics and organizational change
current_usage_summary: Adoption follows three constituencies: (1) Platform/security—want audit, least-privilege, kill switch; will fund GAX-like control planes. (2) Integrators/ISVs—follow distribution (MCP directory today); adopt GAX only if customers demand or hosts add ACSP. (3) Individual developers—optimize for speed; resist unless blocked or token costs hurt. MCP momentum (300M SDK downloads/month, Apr 2026) means GAX wins as governance layer on top, not replacement, for 5+ years. People adapt when pain exceeds switching cost—breaches, token bills, compliance audits accelerate; solo devs adapt slowly.
typical_stack: Change management: security policy → platform engineering → developer enablement. Training: update AGENTS.md templates, cap self-service portal, runbooks for break-glass.
auth_and_identity_model: Org shift from 'credentials on laptop' to 'capabilities per task' mirrors zero-trust maturation—familiar to security buyers, alien to developers until enforced.
context_and_token_behavior: FinOps may mandate GAX projection when MCP tool costs show on invoices—economic driver alongside security.
operational_pain_points: Shadow IT: developers re-enable raw MCP/CLI to avoid caps. Incomplete manifest coverage erodes trust. Vendor fragmentation if every SaaS ships own gaxd flavor.
developer_experience_friction: Perceived as bureaucracy unless cap mint is one-click and commands feel like gh. Need excellent doc/search UX.
gax_mapping: Position GAX as 'the way your org already wants MCP to work in production' not 'cancel MCP'.
migration_phases: Awareness (security whitepaper) → pilot (one team, GitHub/Jira only) → mandate (CI/production agents) → standard (new hires taught gax first) → optional native ACSP in hosts.
adapter_or_bridge_strategy: Open spec (ACSP) + reference impl + manifest registry prevents vendor lock-in fears.
breaking_changes_and_mitigations: Union of developer backlash and integrator inertia—mitigate grandfather period, open source gaxd core, certify manifests.
infrastructure_changes: New role: agent platform engineer. Budget line for hosted control plane.
deployment_topology: Adoption faster in regulated cloud-first orgs; slower in OSS-heavy startups until acquihire/enterprise sale forces it.
adoption_drivers: SOC2/ISO findings on agent actions; OAuth already approved for MCP; token cost visibility; need one audit stream across Cursor+Claude+custom agents.
adoption_blockers: MCP ecosystem network effects; Anthropic advocacy; lack of IDE-native GAX; build vs buy; 'gh works fine'.
adoption_timeline_estimate: 2026–2027: early adopters, security pilots. 2028–2030: enterprise standard for external SaaS from agents if spec stabilizes. Never full replacement of local shell for file/git on laptop.
historical_parallel: Kubernetes adoption: years of docker-compose coexistence; eventual default for prod, not for every script. OAuth for APIs replaced API keys over ~5 years in SaaS.
